name: Tadalafil Antihypertensive Medication Pooled Safety Extract
population_or_context: Patients from phase II-IV ED and/or BPH tadalafil studies, stratified by concomitant antihypertensive medication use; alpha-blockers excluded from this pooled antihypertensive analysis.
sample_size: 15,030 patients in all placebo-controlled studies and 22,825 patients in all studies.
methods: Integrated pooled safety analysis by indication, antihypertensive use, and number of antihypertensive medications.
population_profile: ED and/or BPH trial populations, including patients on antihypertensive medications; indirect for healthy men age 30 to 50 and does not cover alpha-blocker co-use.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil exposure across phase II-IV ED/BPH studies, including as-needed and daily regimens as represented in pooled datasets.
indication_context: On-label ED/BPH clinical safety analysis relevant to off-label cardiovascular interaction framing.
study_design: Pooled analysis of placebo-controlled and all-study clinical trial datasets.
comparator_context: Tadalafil versus placebo within placebo-controlled trials, stratified by antihypertensive medication use.
safety_findings: Hypotension-related TEAE incidence was 0.6% to 1.5% and MACE incidence was 0.0% to 1.0% across all indications in placebo-controlled studies; no significant tadalafil-placebo MACE differences were reported with or without antihypertensive medication use.
directness_to_target_population: Moderate for men age 30 to 50 who take common antihypertensives, but limited by ED/BPH trial selection and exclusion of alpha-blockers.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Pooled across heterogeneous studies; industry involvement; trial populations may exclude high-risk patients; abstract-only extraction for first baseline.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil safety or interaction evidence; transfer from other PDE5 inhibitors is not needed, but monitored trial or clinical-population context limits off-label healthy-user risk estimates.
